name: Yelp
overview:
Yelp is a local business discovery platform where users can search for restaurants, shops, and services near
them, read and write reviews, and view photos. The system must support geospatial search (find businesses
within a radius), full-text search (search by name or category), review aggregation (compute average ratings),
and serve personalized recommendations. The system handles 100M+ businesses and 200M+ reviews
globally.

inScope: Search businesses by location, category, and keyword
View business details, photos, and reviews
Personalized recommendations based on user history

items: Search query latency <200ms p99 for geo+text+rating combined queries
Review writes reflected in search index within 60 seconds
99.9% availability; brief search degradation is tolerable over incorrect results
Geospatial queries must scale to 100M+ businesses without full-table scans
Average rating updates must be consistent — concurrent reviews must not cause incorrect counts

entities.name: Business
entities.schema:
{
  business_id: UUID,
  name: string,
  category: string,

  address: string,
  location: { lat, lng },
  phone: string,
  hours: map<day, {open, close}>,
  avg_rating: float,
  review_count: int,
  photos: [string]
}

entities.name: Review
entities.schema:
{
  review_id: UUID,
  business_id: UUID,
  user_id: string,
  rating: int,  // 1-5
  text: string,
  photos: [string],
  created_at: timestamp,
  useful_count: int,
  funny_count: int,
  cool_count: int
}

endpoints.response:
{ businesses: [{ business_id, name, avg_rating, distance_km, category, is_open
}], total }

approaches.title: SQL with Bounding Box
approaches.description: Filter businesses using a bounding box: WHERE lat BETWEEN lat1 AND lat2 AND lng BETWEEN lng1 AND lng2. Bounding box returns businesses in a square, not a circle. Requires post-filtering. At 100M businesses, even with indexes, this is slow.
approaches.tradeoff: Returns square area (not circle); slow at 100M businesses.

approaches.title: PostGIS ST_DWithin
approaches.description: Use PostGIS ST_DWithin for accurate radius queries. Spatial index (GiST) makes queries fast. Handles 100M businesses with proper indexing. But combining geospatial filter with text search and rating sort requires complex query planning.
approaches.tradeoff: Complex query planning for combined geo+text+rating queries; PostgreSQL scaling limits.

approaches.title: Elasticsearch with Geo Distance Query
approaches.description: Index businesses in Elasticsearch with geo_point field. Use geo_distance query for radius filter, combined with full-text search and rating sort. Elasticsearch handles all three dimensions (geo, text, rating) in a single query. Horizontal sharding scales to 100M+ businesses. Business data is synced from PostgreSQL to Elasticsearch via Kafka.
approaches.tradeoff: Elasticsearch adds operational complexity; sync lag between PostgreSQL and Elasticsearch.

approaches.title: Compute Average on Every Query
approaches.description: SELECT AVG(rating) FROM reviews WHERE business_id = ? At 200M reviews, this query is slow even with indexes. Computing average on every business page load is wasteful.
approaches.tradeoff: Slow for businesses with many reviews; wasteful computation.

approaches.title: Denormalized avg_rating in Business Record
approaches.description: Store avg_rating and review_count in the Business record. Update atomically when a new review is added. avg_rating = (avg_rating × review_count + new_rating) / (review_count + 1). This is O(1) and avoids aggregation queries. The update is done in a PostgreSQL transaction to ensure consistency.
approaches.tradeoff: Denormalized data requires careful update logic; concurrent reviews can cause race

components.name: Search API
components.responsibility: Handles business search queries; queries Elasticsearch; returns ranked results

components.name: Business Service
components.responsibility: Manages business CRUD; syncs to Elasticsearch via Kafka

components.name: Review Service
components.responsibility: Handles review creation; updates avg_rating; publishes to Kafka

components.name: PostgreSQL
components.responsibility: Source of truth for businesses and reviews; ACID transactions

components.name: Redis Cache
components.responsibility: Caches popular business pages and search results; TTL = 5 minutes

components.name: Recommendation Service
components.responsibility: Personalized business recommendations based on user history

components.name: Kafka
components.responsibility: Syncs business/review updates to Elasticsearch and recommendation model

questions.title: 2. Review Spam Detection
questions.content:
Yelp has a sophisticated review spam filter that removes fake reviews. The filter uses: (1) User account age
and activity (new accounts with few friends are suspicious), (2) Review velocity (many reviews in a short time),
(3) IP address clustering (multiple reviews from the same IP), (4) Text similarity (copy-pasted reviews), (5)
Business relationship (reviewer is connected to business owner). Filtered reviews are hidden but not deleted.

questions.title: 3. Photo Storage and Processing
questions.content:
Business and review photos are uploaded to S3 via presigned URLs. A Lambda function triggers on upload,
resizes photos to multiple dimensions (thumbnail 100x100, medium 400x400, large 1200x1200), and stores
them back in S3. Photos are served via CloudFront CDN. The CDN caches popular business photos at edge
nodes for fast delivery.

questions.title: 4. Search Ranking
questions.content:
Business search results are ranked by a combination of: (1) Relevance score (Elasticsearch BM25 for text
queries), (2) Distance (closer businesses ranked higher), (3) Rating (higher-rated businesses ranked higher),
(4) Review count (more reviews = more trustworthy), (5) Recency (recently reviewed businesses ranked
higher). The ranking weights are tuned using A/B testing on click-through rates.

questions.title: 5. Personalized Recommendations
questions.content:
The Recommendation Service uses collaborative filtering to suggest businesses based on the user's review
history. Users who reviewed similar businesses are considered similar. The model is trained weekly on the full
review history. Recommendations are pre-computed for active users and stored in Redis. For new users (cold
start), recommendations are based on popular businesses in their area.

design.diagram:
Yelp — System Design
Search · Review · Rank · Recommend
User

Search API
Review Svc
Elasticsearch
geo+text index
Kafka
sync events
PostgreSQL
source of truth

search results
Rec Svc

S3
photos
GET /search
POST /reviews
cache check
geo+text query
write review
sync event
update index
 train model
upload photo
keyFlows: ① User searches → Search API → check Redis cache → Elasticsearch geo+text
② Elasticsearch: geo_distance + BM25 text + rating sort → ranked results
③ User writes review → Review Svc → PostgreSQL (update avg_rating)
④ PostgreSQL change → Kafka → Elasticsearch index update
⑤ Recommendation Svc: collaborative filtering → pre-computed recs in Redis

whatToStudyFurther.content:
Expert Note: Study Elasticsearch geo queries in depth: the geo_point field type, geo_distance and
geo_bounding_box queries, and how to combine them with full-text search using bool queries.
Expert Note: Learn about review spam detection: how Yelp's recommendation software works, the features
used to identify fake reviews, and the legal challenges of removing reviews.
Expert Note: Understand denormalized aggregates: when to use denormalized counters vs. real-time
aggregation, how to handle concurrent updates safely, and how to reconcile denormalized data with the
source of truth.
Expert Note: Study collaborative filtering for local recommendations: how to handle the sparsity problem
(most users review few businesses), and how to incorporate geographic constraints.
Expert Note: Learn about Elasticsearch cluster architecture: how shards and replicas work, how to size
shards for 100M+ documents, and how to handle index updates without downtime.
